message:
fix(engine): harden content hashes to cover all semantic fields and sort non-deterministic inputs

Twelve modules had content hashes computed from only a subset of struct
fields, allowing silent field tampering or non-deterministic hash output:

- adversarial_supremacy_synthesis: SyntheticWorkload hash adds size_bytes,
  complexity_score, epoch; DecisionReceipt hash adds search_budget_used,
  epoch, sorted counterexamples
- artifact_compression_pipeline: compressed hash includes sizes
- benchmark_coverage_saturation_gate: sort blocking_reasons and
  recommendations before hashing
- bifurcation_boundary_scanner: hash includes full point content,
  warnings, preemptive actions, regime summary (all sorted)
- hardware_code_layout_governance: hash violation content, not count
- metadata_substrate_governance: hash violation details, portability
  score, targets covered/missing
- rollback_safemode_synthesizer: hash kind, sorted rules, constraints
- self_adjusting_resolution_graph: sort affected_modules before hashing
- shipped_path_parity_verifier: sort test_cases by ID before hashing
- containment_executor: debug_assert placeholder sentinel hash
- law_promotion_pack: test no factory returns placeholder hash
- migration_kit: manifest hash covers all fields; expect() on serialize

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
